DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: One control channel in each valve control unit may be inoperative provided manual cabin pressure control is verified to operate normally on both outflow valves before each departure.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Verify manual cabin pressure control operates normally on both outflow valves (AMM DDG 21-31-01).

ListItem.Para: Set the Pressurization Panel (P5) FWD OUTFLOW VALVE and AFT OUTFLOW VALVE switches to MAN.
Hold both manual switches CLOSE until the EICAS outflow valve position indicator reads CL and confirm the forward and aft outflow valve doors are closed.
Hold both manual switches OPEN until the EICAS outflow valve position indicator is in the 1-o’clock position and confirm the forward and aft outflow valve doors are full open.
Set the Pressurization Panel (P5) FWD OUTFLOW VALVE and AFT OUTFLOW VALVE switches to AUTO.
For extended overwater flight, manual cabin pressure control is verified to operate normally on both outflow valves.

Cargo compartments remain empty or contain only non flammable/combustible ballast, empty cargo handling equipment (ballast may be loaded in ULDs), or fly away kits (excluding items such as cans of hydraulic fluid, cleaning solvents, batteries, capacitors, chemical generators, etc).
Flight is conducted unpressurized.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Establish airplane in unpressurized flight configuration, and for extended overwater flight, verify manual cabin pressure control operates normally on both outflow valves (AMM DDG 21-31-01).

ListItem.Para: For extended overwater flight with manual pressurization control operational:
Set the Pressurization Panel (P5) FWD OUTFLOW VALVE and AFT OUTFLOW VALVE switches to MAN.
Hold both manual switches CLOSE until the EICAS outflow valve position indicator reads CL and confirm the forward and aft outflow valve doors are closed.
Hold both manual switches OPEN until the EICAS outflow valve position indicator is in the 1-o’clock position and confirm the forward and aft outflow valve doors are full open.
Leave the Pressurization Panel (P5) FWD OUTFLOW VALVE and AFT OUTFLOW VALVE switches in MAN.
For manual pressure control inoperative (extended overwater flight prohibited), remove the associated outflow valve.
Deactivate the forward and aft lower cargo compartment smoke detection system.
For forward lower cargo compartment smoke detection system, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag.
SMOKE DETECTOR- FWD CARGO 1A
SMOKE DETECTOR- FWD CARGO 1B
SMOKE DETECTOR- FWD CARGO 2A
SMOKE DETECTOR- FWD CARGO 2B
For aft lower cargo compartment smoke detection system, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag.
SMOKE DETECTOR- AFT CARGO 1A
SMOKE DETECTOR- AFT CARGO 1B
SMOKE DETECTOR- AFT CARGO 2A
SMOKE DETECTOR- AFT CARGO 2B

Note.Para: The DET FIRE CARGO FWD and DET FIRE CARGO AFT advisory messages, and the status messages, for all cargo fire detectors will be displayed.

ListItem.Para: Deactivate the crew/attendant rest(s) closed.
Remove all baggage and personal items from the associated crew/attendant rest.

Note.Para: Blankets, pillows and other items normally used in the crew rest may remain.

ListItem.Para: Close the associated crew/attendant rest entrance door.

Note.Para: The CABIN ALTITUDE AUTO caution message will be displayed.
The DET FIRE CARGO FWD and DET FIRE CARGO AFT advisory messages will be displayed.

ListItem.Para: Flight is conducted unpressurized.

Cargo compartments remain empty or contain only non flammable/combustible ballast, empty cargo handling equipment (ballast may be loaded in ULDs), or fly away kits (excluding items such as cans of hydraulic fluid, cleaning solvents, batteries, capacitors, chemical generators, etc), pallets, and cargo restraint components.
For flight with passengers:
Limit enroute altitude to 10,000 feet.
To avoid passenger discomfort, limit climb (after takeoff obstacle clearance has been assured) and descent rates to 500 fpm.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Added a new step 4 for flight without passenger above 14,000 feet.

ListItem.Para: For flight without passengers above 14,000 feet:

ListItem.rev:revHighlight: Added a new guidance to step 4 for flight without passenger above 14,000 feet.
ListItem.Para: Deactivate the passenger oxygen system.
The crew rest areas are not available for inflight use. Remove personal items and do not enter except for inspections by crew members.
For augmented flight crew operations, flight crew duty periods should be adjusted appropriately.
